output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here’s How to Get It Back Fast
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights and how to write a demand letter that gets your money back before it's too late.
content_outline: The Real Reason Your Landlord Isn’t Returning Your Deposit
Is My Landlord Allowed to Keep My Deposit? Know Your Tenant Rights
What Your Landlord Can (and Cannot) Deduct From Your Security Deposit
Security Deposit Not Returned: State Deadlines and Late Penalties
How to Write a Security Deposit Dispute Letter That Gets Results
What to Do Next If Your Landlord Still Refuses to Pay
key_points: Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14–30 days and provide an itemized list of deductions; failure to do so often forfeits their right to keep any of it.
Landlords cannot deduct for normal wear and tear—only for actual damage beyond ordinary use or unpaid rent.
A formal, state-specific demand letter creates legal urgency and is often the fastest way to recover funds without going to small claims court.
DepositBack automates legally compliant security deposit demand letters so tenants can escalate recovery in minutes, not weeks.
output.cta: Stop waiting. Use DepositBack to create your state-specific security deposit demand letter today and force your landlord to take you seriously.
output.target_audience: Problem-aware US renters who are frustrated because their landlord is withholding, delaying, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposit.
